name: Lagoons Route
name_original: Casa Fusta, San Carlos de la Rápita, Ullals de Baltasar – El Poblenou del Delta – ruta circular
description: Explore the Ebro Delta on this 19.1-mile moderate touring cycling route, passing Ullals de Baltasar and El Poblenou del Delta.
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes in Delta De L`Ebre benefit from the region's flat topography, making it suitable for cyclists of all skill levels. The landscape is characterized by extensive rice fields, which transition in color throughout the year, and numerous lagoons such as L'Encanyissada and La Tancada. Over 200 kilometers of dedicated bike paths facilitate access to these natural features, including routes along the Ebro River and repurposed old railway lines. The coastal areas feature sandy beaches and dune systems, offering unique cycling experiences. Best no traffic touring cycling routes in Delta De L`Ebre The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Lagoons Route, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through the region's distinctive wetland lagoons. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Lakes With Waterbirds – Ebro Delta loop from l'Encanyissada, an easy 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This route offers opportunities for birdwatching around the l'Encanyissada lagoon. Local touring cyclists also love the Lo Passador Bridge – Path Through the Rice Fields loop from Illa de Buda, a 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail leading through vast rice fields, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Touring cycling in Delta De L`Ebre is defined by flat terrain, extensive rice fields, and coastal paths along sandy beaches. The network offers options for easy family routes as well as longer, moderate excursions. The routes in Delta De L`Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Delta De L`Ebre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic touring cycling routes are available in Delta De L`Ebre?
A: There are 18 dedicated no-traffic touring cycling routes in Delta De L`Ebre featured in this guide. The flat topography of the Delta makes it ideal for easy, extended rides, with over 200 kilometers of dedicated bike paths throughout the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for no-traffic touring cycling in Delta De L`Ebre?
A: The Delta de l'Ebre offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly recommended for comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery, such as the rice fields transitioning from rich earthy tones to vibrant greens. Summers can be hot, but the coastal routes offer refreshing breezes.

Q: Are there easy, family-friendly no-traffic cycling routes in Delta De L`Ebre?
A: Yes, the Delta de l'Ebre is exceptionally family-friendly due to its flat terrain and extensive network of dedicated bike paths. 14 of the routes in this guide are rated as easy. A great option for families is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes With Waterbirds – Ebro Delta loop from l'Encanyissada&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.1 km long and offers excellent birdwatching opportunities.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the no-traffic cycling routes?
A: The Delta de l'Ebre is a renowned birdwatching haven, hosting 70% of Spain's bird species. You'll frequently spot flamingos, especially around lagoons like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3236684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tancada Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes pass by observation towers and hides, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/979695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tancada Birdwatching Hide&lt;/a&gt;, providing optimal viewing experiences. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974859424/lagoons-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoons Route&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for wildlife enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, many of the no-traffic routes in Delta De L`Ebre are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31892252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lo Passador Bridge – Path Through the Rice Fields loop from Illa de Buda&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic 28.7 km loop through the iconic rice fields.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see along the routes?
A: The Delta de l'Ebre offers several unique landmarks. You can cycle to the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/802480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fangar Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, explore the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6278799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí del Trabucador (Punta de la Banya Sand Road)&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the natural monument of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullals de Baltasar&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass by various birdwatching hides and viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/979684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Carreter&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Delta De L`Ebre?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Delta De L`Ebre, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 166 ratings. Reviewers often praise the region's flat, car-free paths, the stunning natural beauty of the rice fields and lagoons, and the abundant birdwatching opportunities.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Ebro River or its mouth?
A: Yes, several routes trace the Ebro River's path towards its mouth, offering unique perspectives of Spain's longest river meeting the Mediterranean. While not explicitly listed in the routes provided, the region's extensive network of bike paths includes trails that follow the river, providing scenic views and access to areas where boat tours are available.

Q: Can I rent bicycles in Delta De L`Ebre?
A: Yes, bicycle rental services are widely available throughout the Delta de l'Ebre, making it convenient for visitors who don't bring their own bikes. You'll find rental shops in many of the towns and villages within the region, often near popular cycling starting points.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the famous rice fields?
A: Absolutely. The Delta de l'Ebre is famous for its vast rice fields, which cover approximately 22,000 hectares. Many no-traffic cycling routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31892252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lo Passador Bridge – Path Through the Rice Fields loop from Illa de Buda&lt;/a&gt;, wind directly through these fields, offering a dynamic visual experience that changes with the seasons.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty no-traffic routes for a slightly longer ride?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a slightly longer ride with a moderate challenge, there are 3 such routes in this guide. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tancada Birdwatching Hide – Vía Anita Bike Path loop from MónNatura Delta de l'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 34.9 km loop that combines birdwatching with a good distance.
tips: A place of peace and a perfect place to see different species of birds. To the left of the viewpoint you can see the water channel that runs parallel to the greenway that goes around the lagoons. To the right and in the background, you can see the silhouette of the Montsiá mountain range.

Located in an unparalleled setting, the outcrops of Baltassar are freshwater springs, surrounded by flora dominated by eucalyptus. The landscape is unique, as well as the wildlife. If you're on a road bike, you might have to push it for a few moments, but it's definitely worth the trip!

The viewpoint is located at the junction point of the two parts of l'Encanyissada. It is a great place to see birds - the Ebro Delta has become an international ornithological landmark in the southern Mediterranean.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 30.8 kilometers long and generally takes around 2 hours and 6 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. This duration can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly flat with a total elevation gain of about 70 meters, making it generally easy. You'll find mostly paved surfaces, but some segments, especially around the Ullals de Baltasar, might be unpaved and could occasionally require you to push your bike. Overall, it's suitable for touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter diverse and picturesque scenery. Highlights include the unique freshwater springs of Ullals de Baltasar, the Mirador del Carreter, and the Mirador del Través. You'll also see the characteristic landscapes of the Ebro Delta, including lagoons like Encanyissada, traditional white houses in El Poblenou del Delta, and opportunities for sea views on sandy roads leading to beaches like Trabucador.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while cycling?
faqs.answer: The Ebro Delta is renowned for its birdlife, so you'll have excellent opportunities for birdwatching, especially around lagoons like Encanyissada from designated lookout towers. The Ullals de Baltasar area also hosts unique flora and fauna specific to its freshwater ecosystem.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: A convenient starting and stopping point with services is near Casa Fusta. You should be able to find parking options in the vicinity of Casa Fusta or in nearby villages like El Poblenou del Delta or San Carlos de la Rápita.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Ebro Delta Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially in natural areas to protect wildlife. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb the local ecosystem or other cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Ebro Delta offers a tranquil cycling experience year-round. However, spring and autumn are often preferred for milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be hot, while winter can be cooler but still pleasant for cycling.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route in the Ebro Delta Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route within the Parc Natural del Delta de l'Ebre. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and help preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camí de Sant Jaume, Ruta de les llacunes, Amposta - Ullals - Casa de Fusta, and la Ràpita - Casa de Fusta, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can find services and amenities near Casa Fusta and in the charming village of El Poblenou del Delta. These locations offer opportunities for refreshments or a meal, allowing you to refuel during your ride.

faqs.question: Can I rent a bicycle near the starting point of the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, near Casa Fusta, you can find services such as bicycle rentals, making it a convenient option if you don't bring your own bike. This area serves as a good hub for starting your cycling adventure.

waypoints.name: Small White Building in the Rice Fields, Ebro Delta
